zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Jardine Erskine Countess of Balmuto於1354年1月1日出生在英國蘇格蘭培爾時，她的父親Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto47歲，母親Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,46歲。她與James Rutherfurd結婚。她去世於1419年11月20日在蘇格蘭培爾享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: Erskine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Erskine in Renfrewshire, first recorded in 1226 in the form Erskyn.
History: A family of this name have held a number of Scottish titles, including the earldoms of Mar, Buchan, Kellie, and Rosslyn.

variants.name: Erskin
meanings: Scottish (central Scotland and Fife): variant of   Erskine  . This form of the surname is now more common in the US.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Jardine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish (both of Norman origin), and French: topographic or nickname from Old French jardin ‘garden’, for someone who lived near or worked in a garden. Compare Garden .

variants.name: Garden
meanings: Anglo-Norman: Wardeyn, a guard, a keeper of a gate

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Jardine Erskine Countess of Balmuto was born on 1 January 1354, in Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto, was 47 and her mother, Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,, was 46. She married James Rutherfurd. She died on 20 November 1419, in Perthshire, Scotland, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Jardine Erskine Countess of Balmuto est née le 1 janvier 1354, à Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,, avait 46 ans. Elle a épousé James Rutherfurd. Elle est décédée le 20 novembre 1419, à Perthshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret Jardine Erskine Countess of Balmuto am 1. Januar 1354 in Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto 47 und ihre Mutter Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland, 46. Sie hat James Rutherfurd geheiratet. Sie starb am 20. November 1419 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Perthshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Jardine Erskine Countess of Balmuto è nata il il 1 gennaio 1354 nel Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto, aveva 47 anni e sua madre, Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,, aveva 46 anni. Lei sposato James Rutherfurd. Morì il 20 novembre 1419, nel Perthshire, Scotland, all'età di 65 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Jardine Erskine Countess of Balmuto(이)가 1354년 1월 1일 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,(은)는 46세였다. 그녀는 James Rutherfurd(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1419년 11월 20일 스컷랜드 퍼스실르에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Jardine Erskine Countess of Balmuto nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1354, em Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,, tinha 46 anos. Ela casou-se com James Rutherfurd. Ela faleceu em 20 de novembro de 1419, em Perthshire, Scotland, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Jardine Erskine Countess of Balmuto родилась 1 января 1354 г. в местности пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,, 46. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Rutherfurd. Она умерла 20 ноября 1419 г. в местности пиртшир, Шотландия в возрасте 65 лет.

Se casó con James Rutherfurd. Murió el 20 de noviembre de 1419, en Perthshire, Scotland, a la edad de 65 años.
